title: <div>Forced to flee I fled the fatherland for a further land to father my own la
content:
Forced to flee I fled the fatherland for a further land to father my own land led by the word of Apollo out the mouth of his oracle. Either I found this old twisted oracle or this old twisted oracle found me, it mattered not. He grabbed me with shallow veined hands, fingers wrapped around my collars just as the night wrapped herself around the last dying outstretches of light. The oracle's pupils dilated into spiraled pinpricks, his fingers forcefully bent at every joint and knuckle, wrist twitching, mouth agape and waiting to receive transmission:

Follow the marked heifer, he said, follow the marked heifer until he lies.
There bury the fangs, he said, bury the fangs in the dirt.
Establish Thebes, he said, establish Thebes where the marked heifer lies.

The few men afforded to me by my once upon a time father to find his once upon a time daughter hung tight as we fled, as we followed the marked heifer across familiar lands into less and less so until we stood in a great vastness surrounded by unfamiliarity.

The heifer lied, the oracle did not.

Sleep was upon me so strongly then. But, I awoke to the slithering sounds of my noble men choking, bones cracking--a gargantuan snake, standing at least six men tall, ensnared three of my men--the others in differing levels of sleepiness attempted to arm themselves, loading their weapons to rain fire on the beast that reigned bloody violence on us. I did the same, fingering the clips of butterflied bullets in my waistband, but with a crook of the neck and the blink of an eye half of my men were swallowed whole or broken dead on the floor. Chaos and darkness permitted me from finding my gun, but the snake found me, quickly wrapping it's smooth thick body around my waist. It coiled me onto the ground, thrashing itself about as I thrashed free an arm to reach for the blade in my boot. It turned its face to face me, breaking open its lockjaw mouth to swallow me whole. I stared into the vastness of its bestial kingdom and as it descended upon me from above I jammed the blade right between its two crystalline fangs. His warmth spewed down my wrist and arm, his body tightened before it slackened.
